selected_important_turn.text: Okay, maybe we can split the water and then divy up the rest. What else are you interested in?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first explicit move that shifts the interaction from two competing claims on water toward a cooperative frame. It sets the tone for whether the negotiation will stay integrative or slip back into zero-sum bargaining.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The proposal is cooperative but vague — it suggests splitting water and then 'divvy up the rest' without concrete numbers or mentioning firewood. That vagueness left room for the opponent to treat the leftover items competitively (they later asked for all the firewood). The turn could have more clearly defined how all items might be allocated or asked for the other's full priorities to reduce later friction.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Be explicitly concrete and information-seeking at the same time: propose a clear numeric split for water and ask about the other items' relative importance (including firewood). Also validate their need for water to reduce defensiveness.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — clearer structure opens room for integrative trades and prevents inefficient back-and-forth.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — explicit proposals make tradeoffs transparent and easier to justify.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — concreteness reduces ambiguity and accelerates convergence.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — validating the other's need and asking preferences builds trust and lowers defensiveness.

value2reason.Low: I don't want my fire to burn out during our overnight!
value2reason.Medium: I'm a big guy and need a lot of calories -- it can make planning the right amount of food difficult because I'm always hungry.
value2reason.High: I can get really thirsty after doing all my outdoor activities.

value2reason.Low: We recently cut down a dead tree and brought the wood with us.
value2reason.Medium: I might need some extra in case the soup doesn't work out.
value2reason.High: I am going to try to make a soup recipe that I found, so I need to boil a bunch of vegetables.

dialogue.text: Hi! I'm really hoping to get a lot of water. I love outdoor activities like hiking and kayaking so I'll need to stay hydrated on the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hi! I really need water also. I'm trying out a few new recipes, and they call for a lot of water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Okay, maybe we can split the water and then divy up the rest. What else are you interested in?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm focused on water since I need it for my soup. Let's split the water evenly and then trade for food or firewood based on what you need most. How does that sound?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to split the water evenly—1.5 each, but since we can’t split packages, let’s do 2 and 1. What’s your priority for food and firewood? I’m flexible but need to ensure I get enough water for my activities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'll take 2 water packages. For food, I'd prefer 2 packages, and you can have 1. For firewood, I'll take 1, and you get 2. This balances our needs and ensures I have enough for my soup.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 2 water packages, but I need to make sure I get at least 2 food packages to keep up my energy. Firewood is less important to me—how about we adjust the split to 2 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood for me? That way I’m covered for hydration and calories.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 2 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood for you. That gives you enough for your soup and me enough food. Let’s finalize this split and move on.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed! I’ll take 2 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood. You get the remaining 1 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood. Safe and hydrated camping ahead!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
